targets.name: Buddy Carter
targets.reason: Health committee member (Energy and Commerce) + Professional background: pharmacist + Republican — cross-aisle potential + Fence-sitter — persuadable
targets.recommended_approach: Appeal to their scientific/medical training. Share peer-reviewed data and emphasize evidence-based policy. Frame as protecting professional integrity.

targets.name: Dave McCormick
targets.reason: Health committee member (HELP) + Professional background: PhD + Republican — cross-aisle potential + Fence-sitter — persuadable
targets.recommended_approach: Appeal to their scientific/medical training. Share peer-reviewed data and emphasize evidence-based policy. Frame as protecting professional integrity.

targets.name: Megan Blanksma
targets.reason: Health committee chair (Health & Welfare (Chair)) + Republican — cross-aisle potential + Fence-sitter — persuadable
targets.recommended_approach: Focus on their committee influence. Provide constituent health data for their district. Emphasize the committee's role in protecting public health.

targets.name: John Cornyn
targets.recommended_approach: Frame science policy as individual liberty and parental rights. Emphasize informed consent and government transparency over mandates.

targets.name: Bernie Moreno
targets.recommended_approach: Frame science policy as individual liberty and parental rights. Emphasize informed consent and government transparency over mandates.

targets.name: Adam Schiff
targets.recommended_approach: Thank them for their support. Ask them to co-sponsor pro-science bills and use their influence to bring colleagues on board.
